title: Legacy001 GBDT control expert
summary: Add one GBDT control expert, preferably XGBoost CUDA or LightGBM CPU, without stacking or broad tuning.
rationale: A single GBDT control tests whether tree-based decision boundaries complement the RealMLP baseline. On this task LightGBM should remain CPU-only because GPU/CUDA backends have shown native failures, while XGBoost has a straightforward CUDA histogram path.
implementation_hint: Patch in exactly one GBDT expert. Prefer XGBoost with tree_method='hist' and device='cuda' when GPU is enabled; if using LightGBM, keep it CPU-only and do not set GPU/CUDA device parameters. Use the same folds, report standalone OOF AUC and simple average with RealMLP, and avoid adding CatBoost, stacking, or calibration.
expected_effect: Identify whether a GBDT expert adds complementary ranking signal before investing in a larger ensemble.
risk: GBDT may be weaker than RealMLP and can add runtime; LightGBM GPU should be avoided on this task due prior native aborts and bin-size issues.
